claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for my child Alex. I'm asking about one child.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you some questions.

First, how old is Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 20 years old, I need to ask: Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

(For Child Benefit purposes, you can normally only claim for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me also confirm: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16 years old. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, the age eligibility criteria are not met.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if they were to start approved education or training while still under 20), you would have been able to claim, but at 20 years old without being in education or training, Child Benefit is not available.

Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, the age eligibility criteria are not met. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet these age and education requirements, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, the age eligibility criteria are not met. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet these age and education requirements, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
